---
title: "PhillyStat360 — 04f: Per-parcel explanations (TreeSHAP, R port)"
output: html_document
---

R port of `code/python/04f_local_explanations.ipynb`. Generates per-parcel
SHAP-like contributions for the top-N flagged parcels using the `treeshap`
package (R analog of Python's `shap.TreeExplainer`).

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

```{r libs}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(ranger)
  # treeshap and ranger.unify
  if (!requireNamespace("treeshap", quietly = TRUE)) {
    message("Install treeshap: install.packages('treeshap') (or remotes::install_github('ModelOriented/treeshap')")
  }
  library(treeshap)
})

ROOT       <- here::here()
DATA_PATH  <- file.path(ROOT, "data")
PY_PATH    <- file.path(ROOT, "data_py")
OUT_PATH   <- PY_PATH
GRAPH_PATH <- file.path(ROOT, "graphs", "python")
dir.create(GRAPH_PATH, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
SEED <- 42
```

## 1. Load model, features, predictions

```{r load}
rf <- readRDS(file.path(PY_PATH, "model_rf_final.rds"))
feat_full <- read_csv(file.path(DATA_PATH, "features_residential.csv"),
                      show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  mutate(parcel_number = str_trim(as.character(parcel_number)))

X_cols <- rf$forest$independent.variable.names
cat("Features used:", length(X_cols), "\n")

preds <- read_csv(file.path(PY_PATH, "predictions_04b.csv"),
                  show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  mutate(parcel_number = str_trim(as.character(parcel_number)))
```

## 2. Top-N flagged parcels

```{r top-n}
TOP_N <- 200L
top_parcels <- preds |>
  arrange(desc(ensemble_prob)) |>
  head(TOP_N) |>
  left_join(feat_full |> select(parcel_number, all_of(X_cols)),
            by = "parcel_number")
```

## 3. TreeSHAP

```{r treeshap}
X_top <- top_parcels |> select(all_of(X_cols)) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), \(x) replace_na(x, median(x, na.rm = TRUE))))

unified <- treeshap::ranger.unify(rf, as.data.frame(X_top))
shap_obj <- treeshap::treeshap(unified, as.data.frame(X_top), verbose = FALSE)

# shap_obj$shaps is a data frame n × p with SHAP values per feature
shap_mat <- as.matrix(shap_obj$shaps)
cat("SHAP matrix:", nrow(shap_mat), "×", ncol(shap_mat), "\n")
```

## 4. Tidy per-parcel explanation table

```{r tidy}
top_5_per_parcel <- function(i) {
  s <- shap_mat[i, ]
  ord <- order(abs(s), decreasing = TRUE)[1:5]
  tibble(
    parcel_number = top_parcels$parcel_number[i],
    ensemble_prob = top_parcels$ensemble_prob[i],
    ovs = top_parcels$ovs[i],
    feature = colnames(shap_mat)[ord],
    value   = as.numeric(X_top[i, ord]),
    shap    = s[ord],
    direction = ifelse(s[ord] > 0, "pushed up", "pushed down")
  )
}

out <- map_dfr(seq_len(nrow(shap_mat)), top_5_per_parcel)
write_csv(out, file.path(OUT_PATH, "parcel_shap_topflags.csv"))
cat("Wrote", nrow(out), "rows\n")
head(out, 15)
```

## 5. Global summary across top-N

```{r global, fig.width=8, fig.height=6}
mean_abs <- tibble(
  feature = colnames(shap_mat),
  mean_abs = colMeans(abs(shap_mat))
) |> arrange(desc(mean_abs)) |> head(15) |>
  mutate(feature = factor(feature, levels = rev(feature)))

shap_p <- ggplot(mean_abs, aes(mean_abs, feature)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#1f77b4") +
  labs(title = sprintf("Top features driving flags (top %d parcels)", TOP_N),
       x = "mean(|SHAP|)", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal()
ggsave(file.path(GRAPH_PATH, "shap_summary_topflags.png"),
       shap_p, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 200)
shap_p
```
